extraActivities: Live music throughout morning and afternoon
Recharge Zone (sauna, cold plunge, red light therapy)
Food & drinks available all morning and afternoon

preRaceSetup.tuesdayThroughThursday: Course marking and race hub setup

course.overview: Rolling, well-marked 5-mile loop on soft forest trails
course.terrain: Primarily soft trails through pretty forest with a handful of hills

lapStructure.50K: First lap: 1 mile. Laps 2-6: full 5 mile loop

markings.standard: World-renowned course markings, well above industry standard
markings.nighttime: High-grade reflector tape for dark hour races
markings.colorCoding: NOT color-coded per distance. Check intersections for distance indicators
markings.offCourse: If you get off course, you can get back on and continue. Must complete entire course for official finish.

course.policy: Headphones discouraged due to high volume of runners at different paces
course.gpsDownloads: GPX and PDF elevation profile available for download

hippoHaul.format: Carry a concrete hippo (~5-10 lbs) for the full distance
hippoHaul.rules: Whoever carries the hippo the farthest wins. One hippo per participant.

ruckDivision.overview: Ruck challenge/competition with standardized weight requirements
ruckDivision.awards: Top finishers rewarded. Same divisions as regular trail runs. All finishers get medals.
ruckDivision.options: Long course (10K or 10 miles) and less long course (similar distances) available

weightRequirements.weightCheck: Mandatory ruck weigh-in prior to start at timing chip pickup and at finish
weightRules: Weight cannot be consumable or wearable (no food, water, or clothing)
Must be on back in backpack/ruck, NOT on shoulders or chest
Can carry additional gear (food, water, clothing) not counting toward weight requirement

aidStations.overview: Fully supported aid station at start/lap/finish and one on-course aid station
aidStations.finish: Cold sodas and water available at finish line
aidStations.cupless: All races are cupless - bring your own cup or water bottle
leaveNoTrace: Partner of Leave No Trace principles
No toilet paper in woods - use personal baggie and wipes

dropBags.policy: NO formal drop bag service offered
dropBags.alternative: You can leave personal bags around race hub for retrieval at each lap
dropBags.restriction: Do not leave unattended belongings outside the race hub

timing.chipTiming: All races chip-timed with ankle strap
timing.chipReturn: Place chip in bin at finish - head timer collects

gunVsChipTime.gunTime: From mass start time until you cross finish
gunVsChipTime.chipTime: From when you actually cross start line until you cross finish

timing.liveUpdates: Results updated each time runner passes timing mat

dnf.definition: Did Not Finish - runner did not complete registered distance
dnf.policy: Cutoff is strictly enforced. Missing cutoff by even one second results in DNF
dnf.reasoning: Respects safety and fairness to other racers
dnf.medal: Runners who complete shorter distance can request medal for accomplished miles
dnf.cutoffEnforcement: Cannot continue after cutoff - cannot be flexible

overall.format: Handmade awards given at finish line
overall.ceremony: No formal awards ceremony at most events
overall.policy: Cannot mail awards - must pick up at finish

awards.noDubleDoubledipping: Runners who place get highest award possible, then out of contention for other awards
awards.multiRaceChallenge: Special awards for 500, 1000, 1500km or miles at same event

volunteering.value: Essential to race operations - cannot function without volunteers
benefits: Park entry fees and camping fees paid (if role requires overnight)
Volunteer hours exchanged for future race entries

swag.included: World-renowned course markings, unique medal, awesome gear
swag.lateReg: Everyone who registers after shirt cutoff still gets awesome swag
swag.swapPolicy: Can swap swag item after final packet pickup if unsatisfied
swag.premiumOption: Upgrade to nicer swag item available at some events
swag.noSwagOption: Can opt out of swag for discount at registration
swag.shirtCutoff: When shirt count sent to printer - necessary to order in time
swag.colorNote: Shirt colors may vary from planned due to supply chain issues

lotA.name: Parking Lot A
lotA.description: Grass field at Hippo Social Club, closest to race hub

lotB.name: Parking Lot B
lotB.description: Grass field on same side of Limmer Loop, just west of Hippo Social Club

lotC.name: Parking Lot C
lotC.description: Parking lot of Cottonwood Creek Elementary, across Limmer Loop from church

gettingThere.runnerDropOff: Runner drop-off area in parking area for quick drop-off and supply drop
gettingThere.runnerFeePolicy: Runner entrance fees covered by Tejas Trails

camping.nearby: Race course in middle of Hutto with many lodging options nearby

specialEventFees.definition: Parks charge for venue access, land usage, parking, setup
specialEventFees.note: Texas State Parks Passes do NOT cover special event fees

insurance.claims: Submit with official documentation (doctor, clinic, work, etc.)

toPerson.timeline: Must request 1+ week before race day
toPerson.process: Transferor emails registration@eventdatasolutions.com, transferee receives 50% discount code, pays difference
toPerson.conditions: One week or more before race

toEvent.description: Defer to different Tejas Trails event
toEvent.process: Email registration@eventdatasolutions.com with subject 'Deferral to another event'
restrictions: Only to same distance or shorter
Can only defer once - cannot defer a deferral
Must use same distance or shorter
No refund if moving to shorter distance

pregnancyDeferral.eligibility: Pregnant after registration, don't want to race
pregnancyDeferral.process: Submit deferral request with doctor/midwife confirmation before first race day
pregnancyDeferral.benefits: Automatic entry into any of next three Tejas Trails events, same or shorter distance

waitLists.when: Activated when race reaches max capacity
waitLists.process: First in, first out by distance
waitLists.credits: Cancellations receive 75% deferral credit for next running

discounts.policy: Cannot retroactively apply discounts. Check for available discounts before registering
discounts.volunteering: Volunteer hours can be exchanged for future race entries

sellingBibs.reasons: Insurance, safety, competitive integrity, fraud prevention, fairness

cancellationProcess: Text alert if signed up during registration
Posted on Tejas Trails Facebook page
raceCancellations.history: In hundreds of races, only 3 cancellations (extreme weather)

lateRegistration.process: Everyone gets swag even after shirt cutoff
lateRegistration.afterPacketPickup: Receive swag after volunteers are fitted

premiumSwag.availability: Some events allow premium swag upgrade at registration

dnf.shortDistance: Can get medal for shorter distance completed if requested

cutoffs.purpose: Mathematical safety limit for final lap start and course closure

offCourse.policy: Can get back on course and continue. Must complete entire course for official finish.
offCourse.medal: Will receive medal for distance completed if it matches an offered distance

courseRecords.verification: May need to verify with GPS watch if setting record
courseRecords.data: Submit GPS or device data if claiming record

pacers.hippo: Not allowed at Hippo Trail Fest

dogsAllowed.spectators: Can bring dogs to cheer on racers (leashed)

personalInfo.usage: Used for race management. Sometimes shared with sponsors/beneficiaries with notice.
personalInfo.sharing: Only registration platform and Tejas Trails have access

emails.signup: By e-signing waiver, you give permission to add to general email list
emails.preRace: Pre-race specific emails required - contain important info

photos.policy: Photos kept private. Used with permission only

regulatory.cannabis: Tejas Trails takes no official position on personal, responsible cannabis use

general.question: What's included with my race entry?
general.answer: World-renowned course markings, unique medal, race swag (shirt, hat, etc.), all gear needed for aid stations

general.question: Can I swap my swag if I don't like what I get?
general.answer: Yes, you can swap after packet pickup closes, through final packet pickup

general.question: Is there camping at Hippo Trail Fest?
general.answer: No camping on-site, but the race is in Hutto with many nearby lodging options

general.question: Are refunds available?
general.answer: No. Refunds are not permitted under any circumstances. Consider purchasing can't-make-it insurance.

general.question: Can I transfer my race entry to someone else?
general.answer: Yes, if requested 1+ week before race day. Transferee receives 50% discount code. Subject to 50% transfer fee.

general.question: Can I defer to a different event?
general.answer: Yes, on sliding scale: 90+ days = 100%, 30-89 days = 75%, 10-29 days = 50%, 0-9 days = no transfer. $10 admin fee applies.

general.question: Are pacers allowed?
general.answer: No, pacers are not allowed at Hippo Trail Fest

general.question: Can I run with my dog?
general.answer: No, dogs cannot run the race. Spectators can bring leashed dogs to cheer.

general.question: What if I get off course?
general.answer: You can get back on course and continue. Must complete entire course for official finish. Will get medal for distance completed.

general.question: What happens if I miss the cutoff?
general.answer: You'll be marked as DNF (Did Not Finish) even if only seconds over. Cutoffs are strictly enforced for safety/fairness.

general.question: Can I get an early start?
general.answer: No, early starts are not supported

general.question: Is the race rain or shine?
general.answer: Yes, races happen rain or shine. Plan for mud if raining.

general.question: Can I use headphones?
general.answer: Discouraged. High volume of runners at different paces makes it unsafe.

general.question: What if the race gets cancelled?
general.answer: Only in extreme conditions. No refunds, but backup venue/date provided. Check email, text alerts, Facebook for updates.
